description: Replace hardcoded 3-model list with dynamic Hugging Face API call that fetches GGUF models < 3B parameters, fix download URL construction, fix the false completion issue, and add org.json dependency for JSON parsing
steps: Add org.json is already available in Android SDK (android.jar includes org.json.JSONObject/JSONArray), so no dependency needed. Use JSONObject/JSONArray for parsing HF API responses.

In AIModelRepositoryImpl.kt, replace the hardcoded model list in fetchRemoteModels() with a real HTTP call to the Hugging Face API endpoint: GET https://huggingface.co/api/models?filter=gguf&sort=downloads&direction=-1&limit=50. Parse the JSON response to extract model metadata.

Filter the API results to only include models that are likely < 3B parameters. The HF API does not directly return parameter count, but we can filter by looking at the model ID / tags. Use a heuristic: include models whose ID or tags contain indicators of small size (0.5B, 1B, 1.5B, 2B, 3B) OR filter by known file sizes (GGUF Q4_K_M for 3B model is roughly < 2GB). A practical approach: after getting models, fetch the file listing for each model from https://huggingface.co/api/models/{model_id} to find GGUF files, and filter by file size < 2GB (Q4_K_M of 3B is about 1.8GB).

For each model from the API, determine the correct GGUF filename to download. The HF API model detail endpoint (https://huggingface.co/api/models/{model_id}) returns siblings[] with filenames. Look for Q4_K_M .gguf files. If not found, look for Q4_0 or any .gguf file under 2GB.

Fix the download URL construction. The current URL pattern 'https://huggingface.co/{id}/resolve/main/{ggufFileName}' is correct for most repos. But some repos may use different branch names or paths. Keep the current pattern as it works for standard HF repos.

Fix the false-completion issue (Issue 2): The current check 'if (targetFile.exists() && targetFile.length() > 4096)' can be fooled by leftover .tmp files that got renamed or partial downloads from crashes. Add a more robust check: also verify the file is not suspiciously small compared to expected size. If modelInfo.fileSizeBytes is known and > 0, check that targetFile.length() >= modelInfo.fileSizeBytes * 0.9 (allow 10% tolerance for different quant). If the file is much smaller than expected, delete it and re-download.
Handle HTTP redirects properly - OkHttpClient follows redirects by default (followRedirects = true), so this should work. But ensure the download handles 302/303 redirects from HF CDN correctly.
Add proper error messages that indicate what went wrong (e.g., 'Model requires authentication' for 401, 'File not found' for 404).
Update ModelInfo data class if needed to support optional fields from the API (some models may not have exact file sizes until we fetch the file listing).
acceptance_criteria: fetchRemoteModels() makes a real HTTP call to HF API and returns a dynamic list of GGUF models
Only models with GGUF files < 2GB (roughly < 3B parameters in Q4 quantization) are shown
Each model has a valid ggufFileName derived from the actual HF repo file listing
The download URL pattern correctly resolves for standard HF repos
The false-completion bug is fixed - files are validated against expected size before being marked as completed
Error messages are descriptive (404 = file not found, 401 = auth required, etc.)
verification: Read the modified AIModelRepositoryImpl.kt and verify it makes HTTP calls to HF API
Verify the JSON parsing logic correctly extracts model IDs and GGUF filenames
Verify the file existence check now validates file size against expected size
Check that the code compiles syntactically (imports, types, etc. are correct)
findings: Implementation complete. Uses org.json (Android SDK built-in) for parsing HF API responses. Makes individual model detail API calls to get siblings/file sizes since the search endpoint does not include file sizes. Fallback list uses bartowski repos which are known-good public GGUF quantizations. The false-completion fix validates file size >= 90% of expected before marking as complete. Also validates downloaded tmp file size before renaming to final destination. Cannot run Gradle build due to INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network, but code was verified for Kotlin syntax correctness and proper API usage patterns.
